zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Turberville於1374年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根時，她的父親Hamon De Turberville24歲，母親Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn24歲。她於大約1395年與Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1444年在英國享年70歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Turberville于1374年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根时，她的父亲Hamon De Turberville24岁，母亲Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn24岁。她于大约1395年与Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1444年在英国享年70岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Turberville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, probably from Thouberville (Eure), or possibly from Trubleville (Seine-Maritime), both in France. The two may have been confused in surname usage.

variants.name: Torbet
meanings: from the Anglo-Norman and Middle English personal name T(h)urbert, T(h)orbert, a hybrid of Old Norse Thorr ‘Thor’, the name of the god of thunder, + ancient Germanic -berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was probably formed on the Continent; see Turberville .
perhaps in some cases a nickname from Old French and Middle English turbot, turbut ‘turbot’, for someone who caught or sold or resembled this fish.

variants.name: Tuberville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Turberville  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Turville
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire; of Norman origin):
habitational name from Tourville‐la‐Campagne (Eure, France). The placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Thórr + Old French ville ‘farmstead, estate’ (from Latin villa).
occasionally a shortened form of   Turberville  .

variants.name: Troublefield
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Turberville  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Turberville was born in 1374, in Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Hamon De Turberville, was 24 and her mother, Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn, was 24. She married Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle about 1395, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1444, in England, at the age of 70, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Turberville est née en 1374, à Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Hamon De Turberville, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle environ 1395, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1444, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Turberville 1374 in Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hamon De Turberville 24 und ihre Mutter Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn 24. Sie hat um 1395 in England Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1444 im Alter von 70 Jahren in England und wurde in England begraben.

Lei sposato Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle circa 1395, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1444, nel England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Turbervilleが1374年にウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Hamon De Turbervilleは24歳，母Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Brynは24歳であった。約1395年にイギリスでSir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1444年にイギリスで70歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Turberville(이)가 1374년 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hamon De Turberville(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1395년 영국에서 Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1444년 영국에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Turberville nasceu em 1374, em Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Hamon De Turberville, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle aproximadamente 1395, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1444, em Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Turberville родилась в 1374 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Hamon De Turberville было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine verch Thomas Bevon of Bryn, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Maunsell of Oxwich Castle около 1395 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1444 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.
